sourceMovie.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Diary of a Wimpy Kid for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Thor Freudenthal keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Toy Story 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Diary of a Wimpy Kid clicked for you, Toy Story 3 (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Diary of a Wimpy Kid?
faq.answer: Try Toy Story 3, The Simpsons Movie, Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory—each shares DNA with Diary of a Wimpy Kid while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Diary of a Wimpy Kid?
faq.answer: Diary of a Wimpy Kid (2010) is best described as Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Diary of a Wimpy Kid part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Diary of a Wimpy Kid Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
